calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition For Settlement Of First Interim Account, Or Petition For Final Distribution

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Jordan for administrator Brian Phifer (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Final Distribution.

The Court noted that the estate was not ready to close and that petitioner's recent status report included a request for a 120-day continuance. The Court denied the request for a continuance and directed counsel to file a new petition, either for settlement of a first interim account covering the first two years from the date of death through January 2024 with a request to keep the estate open, or a revised petition for final distribution, or a petition for the administrator's first and final account. The instant petition was taken off calendar.

Mr. Jordan reported that an application to appoint a new probate referee had recently been submitted, as the originally assigned referee had passed away.

The Court set a status hearing for July 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 for filing of a new petition.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-08-2025 Continued To Mar-16-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Appearances: Vernon Goins appeared via zoom for Brian Phifer (petitioner).

The Court confirmed no objections to reducing the bond to $500,000.00. Petition to reduce bond is taken under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Saephan. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jun-21-2023 Continued To Jul-10-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Apr-26-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances; Vernon Goins appearing via zoom for Brian Douglas Phifer (petitioner).

The Court indicated that there is no inventory and appraisal and that the letters haven't been picked up. Mr. Goins stated that they estimated estate funds at $2,000,000.00, that his client is unable to obtain a bond and that after their due diligence the value of their assets (retirement account/bank account) are under $500,000.00. Mr. Goins also stated that they attempted to file an amended petition, but it got rejected because there is no pending petition. The Court suggested vacating the order of July 25, 2022 and that Mr. Goins file a new petition. Further discussion was held regarding the possibility of coming in exparte to address an appropriate bond amount. Status of bond or filing of a new petition is set for June 21, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the bond issue is not resolved by then the Court will vacate the July 25, 2023 order. Status of filing of Inventory of Appraisal is off calendar. Status on close of estate that is set for August 21, 2023 is also off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Ateesh Datt; Deputy: Maloney; Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Continued To Apr-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vernon Goins appeared by zoom for Brian Phifer (administrator).
Laura Masataler appeared by court call in propria persona.

Mrs. Masataler stated that she is not being communicated with and that she wants to know what is happening with the estate. Mr. Goins stated that his client has to file an amended petition as he faces challenges when seeking to qualify for a $2,000,000.00 bond, and that he would like to amend the petition to allow his client to serve with a blocked account, or allow the amount of bond to be reduced. Mr. Goins also stated that his client probably qualifies for a bond for half the amount and that he can file his petition in a couple of weeks. Mrs. Masataler stated that her mother has been deceased for a year, that nothing has been done about the bond, and asked what the valuation of the estate is and how they will be communicated with regarding information. Mrs. Masataler also stated that the only way the siblings know about this is because they go to the court's website. The court explained the process of probate and noted that the letters have not been issued because of the problems with the bond. Discussion was held regarding the issue of whether the asset of the estate is a piece of real property in San Francisco, or just money in the bank and a car. Mr. Goins that Laura can call him and that he is willing to keep the lines of communication going. Mrs. Masataler raised the issue of filing a petition to be appointed. The court assures Mrs. Masataler that once this gets going, everything will be in writing. Status in filing of the inventory and appraisal is continued to April 26, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If anyone else wants to file a petition, they can do it within this timeframe.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
